reports.title: Anthropic's AI Can Break Your Bank
reports.subtitle: Mythos finds thousands of zero-day exploits autonomously. Washington called an emergency meeting with bank CEOs. Nobody knows whether to use it or ban it.

reports.title: The Blockade That Is Already Being Traded Away
reports.subtitle: Trump imposed a naval blockade on Iranian ports after Islamabad talks failed. Iran offered a 5-year uranium pause. Markets say both sides want a deal before May.

reports.title: The Court Ordered $133 Billion Back. Now What?
reports.subtitle: The Supreme Court struck down Trump's IEEPA tariffs. A federal court ordered refunds. Trump is already testing a workaround using a different statute.

reports.title: The Fed Has No Good Options Left
reports.subtitle: A Federal Reserve study confirms tariffs drove 3.1% cumulative inflation. Consumer spending is holding. Growth is stalling. The Fed cannot cut rates into a supply shock without making it worse.

reports.title: The Ban That Moves Children Underground
reports.subtitle: Australia's under-16 social media ban has failed: two-thirds of affected teens are still on banned platforms. The UK and Massachusetts are about to copy the same approach.

reports.title: The Arms Transfer That Could Detonate the Xi Summit
reports.subtitle: US intelligence says China is routing air defense systems to Iran. Trump is threatening 50% tariffs. A May summit hangs in the balance. Beijing denies everything.

reports.title: Billions in Tariff Refunds Are Coming. Consumers Won't See a Dollar.
reports.subtitle: The Supreme Court struck down Trump's tariffs and ordered $133 billion in refunds. A CFO survey found most companies plan to keep the money. 86% of CEOs now treat tariffs as permanent anyway.

reports.title: The Surveillance Law Both Parties Hate Is About to Lapse
reports.subtitle: FISA Section 702 expires April 20. The White House wants a clean extension. House Republicans are blocking it for warrant requirements. The military says a lapse would blind US intelligence in an active war.

reports.title: Day 58: Republicans Are Using the Nuclear Option to Fund Immigration Enforcement
reports.subtitle: The DHS shutdown hit two months. The Senate passed a partial reopening that excludes ICE and CBP. Now Republicans are using budget reconciliation to fund immigration agencies for three years, bypassing the filibuster.

reports.title: The Aviation Safety Bill Was Too Weak. Then Congress Weakened It More.
reports.subtitle: 67 people died in the Potomac River crash 15 months ago. The House safety bill needs a two-thirds vote tonight. Victims' families say it lacks strict timelines. The NTSB said the original was 'watered down.'
